diagrams.title: Orthogonal Vectors in R²
diagrams.description: Two perpendicular vectors (1,0) and (0,1) in a 2D coordinate system forming a right angle
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the geometric interpretation of orthogonal vectors - they are perpendicular and meet at 90°.

diagrams.title: Gram-Schmidt Process Steps
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization showing how three non-orthogonal vectors are converted to orthogonal set
elements: u₂ with projection removed onto u₁
u₃ with projections removed onto both u₁ and u₂
Final orthogonal vectors v₁, v₂, v₃
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how each Gram-Schmidt step removes components in previous directions to create orthogonal vectors.

diagrams.title: Orthogonal Decomposition Theorem
diagrams.description: A vector y decomposed into component ŷ in subspace W and component z in orthogonal complement W⊥
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how any vector can be uniquely split into parallel and perpendicular components relative to a subspace.

diagrams.title: Projection onto a Line
diagrams.description: Showing projection of vector u onto vector v in R²
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the projection formula and how to find the component of a vector in a given direction.

diagrams.title: Orthogonal Matrix as Rotation/Reflection
diagrams.description: An orthogonal matrix transforms the unit circle to itself (rotation) or reflects it (determinant -1)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that orthogonal matrices preserve lengths and angles - they represent rotations or reflections.
